name: Python Todo CLI
description: Build a CLI todo app with JSON persistence, argparse, multi-module Python structure, and pytest.
prompt:
Build a new Python command-line todo list application from scratch in the current directory.

Project structure:
  todo.py       — main CLI entrypoint (argparse)
  storage.py    — JSON persistence helpers
  models.py     — Todo dataclass
  test_storage.py — pytest tests for storage
  test_models.py  — pytest tests for the Todo model

1. models.py defines a Todo dataclass with fields:
   - id: int
   - title: str
   - completed: bool (default False)
   - created_at: str (ISO-8601 timestamp string)
   Provide a to_dict() method and a from_dict(data) classmethod.

2. storage.py defines:
   - DEFAULT_PATH = '.todos.json'
   - load_todos(path=DEFAULT_PATH) -> list[Todo]
     If the file is missing or empty, return an empty list.
   - save_todos(todos, path=DEFAULT_PATH) -> None
     Writes the todos as a JSON array using Todo.to_dict().
   - next_id(todos) -> int
     Returns the smallest unused integer id starting at 1.

3. todo.py uses argparse with these subcommands:
   - add TITLE          : add a new todo, print 'added: <id> <title>'
   - list               : print each todo as '[x] 3: Buy milk' (x if completed, space otherwise), one per line. Print nothing if empty.
   - complete ID        : mark id as completed, print 'completed: <id>'. Exit 1 if id not found.
   - remove ID          : delete id from the list, print 'removed: <id>'. Exit 1 if id not found.
   All commands operate on .todos.json in the current working directory.

4. test_models.py covers:
   - Todo defaults (completed=False)
   - to_dict / from_dict round-trip

5. test_storage.py covers:
   - load returns [] when file missing
   - save then load round-trips multiple todos
   - next_id returns 1 for empty list, max+1 for non-empty
   Use pytest's tmp_path fixture to isolate from the real filesystem.

6. Set up the virtual environment and install pytest:
   python3 -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install pytest -q

7. Run the tests:
   .venv/bin/python -m pytest -v

8. Demonstrate the CLI works end-to-end:
   .venv/bin/python todo.py add "buy milk"
   .venv/bin/python todo.py add "call mom"
   .venv/bin/python todo.py list
   .venv/bin/python todo.py complete 1
   .venv/bin/python todo.py list

No stubs. All five files (.py) must be real implementations.
